inline_comments.message: I want to test this commit further, but I love the idea of the YangModelFactory, very neat!
This should be same revision as in other methods, e.g. pass as parameter
use an enum to control the type data being generated, not a string

instead of hardcoding this array I would prefer to see a single constant NUMBER_OF_MODULE_SET_TAGS form that you can generate names and easily support up to 26 tags using tagA-tagB pattern, alternatively we could use tag001-tag999 pattern but that might also affect other testcode. so maybe stick with TagA-tagE

these 'magic' numbers should be extracted our as (private!) constant
NUMBER_OF_MODULES_PER_MODULE_SET = 200
NUMBER_OF_MODULES_NOT_IN_MODULE_SET = 10
yes factory is nice but the data should be produce once upon construction/application start or similar as we discussed in the meeting
you should have had checkstyle issues, this needs to be final
loads of 'final's mssing in this code!
as discussed revision should also depend on tag name!

The @SpringBootApplication annotation itself includes @ComponentScan, which, by default, scans the package where the application class resides (i.e., the org.onap.cps.ncmp.dmi.rest.stub package in this case) and its subpackages.

So unless you want to scan additional packages that aren't in the same package or subpackages as DmiDemoApplication, the explicit @ComponentScan is redundant.
Use it as 1st line of this method
